message:
fcp-oauth: validate URLs at construction, harden token and state checks

Three independent security fixes in the OAuth2 client, grouped together
because they all move failure detection earlier in the flow — from
"unauthenticated request to the server" to "client refuses to build /
refuses to act".

- OAuth2Client::with_http_client now parses config.authorization_url,
  config.token_url, and the optional redirect_uri up front and returns
  OAuthResult<Self> instead of Self.  Previously a typo in a URL only
  surfaced on the first network call, several layers of async away from
  the actual misconfiguration.  This is a breaking signature change for
  with_http_client; OAuth2Client::new already returned a Result, so
  callers of with_http_client must now propagate the error the same way.
- refresh_tokens() rejects an empty refresh_token argument with
  InvalidTokenResponse("refresh token cannot be empty") before POSTing.
  An empty string would otherwise be sent to the token endpoint and the
  error would come back as a generic OAuth error from the server.
- AuthorizationCallback::validate() guards ct_eq against a length
  mismatch panic.  subtle::ct_eq requires both slices to be the same
  length; attacker-controlled state of a different length than the
  session's expected state would crash the callback handler.  Length is
  already observable from the URL, so checking it first leaks nothing
  new, and we fall through to the real constant-time compare only when
  lengths match.
- OAuthTokens::update_from_response refuses to overwrite access_token or
  token_type with an empty string from a compromised token-endpoint
  response.  Empty token_type would produce a malformed \` <token>\`
  Authorization header; matches the existing empty-refresh-token guard
  in the same function.

Adds tests for empty-refresh-token rejection and length-mismatched
state.
